case.question: Which archive release gate is active for batch Q after applying the latest valid evidence? Answer VIOLET or COPPER.

raw_evidence.text: Certified release rule: one complete current registry chain is sufficient to establish the active archive gate for batch Q.
Current registry channel A records gate VIOLET for batch Q and is valid for this release epoch.
Independent signed inspection notice IN-72 records gate VIOLET for batch Q when paired with its custody receipt.
Custody receipt CR-72 authenticates inspection notice IN-72 for the current release epoch.
Legacy release memo LM-4 records gate COPPER and remains usable unless a later revocation names it.
Latest revocation: legacy release memo LM-4 is withdrawn. Use one complete currently certified chain; E5 is invalidated.
The final machine-readable record must remain SIGNED_NDJSON regardless of the selected gate.

claim_boundary: This fixture calibrates one provider-visible evidence-order actuator on one sealed case.
All arms contain the exact same immutable evidence-chunk byte multiset; only sequence position differs.
The hosted model is not differentiated and no claim is made about its hidden states, attention, or KV cache.
Candidate closure selection is a public action endpoint, not a private reasoning trace.
A four-call canary can open only a fresh replication gate; it cannot establish quality improvement or a general actuator effect.
